source_jobs.title: Technical Solution Analyst (TSA)
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

GridUnity is the industry leader in interconnection life cycle management, delivering cutting-edge software solutions that eliminate bottlenecks in the interconnection process and accelerate the transition to a more affordable, sustainable, and resilient energy future.

Our Mission

We unite all grid interconnection stakeholders on a single platform that converts centralized data into actionable intelligence and accelerates the transition to a reliable, sustainable, and cost-efficient electric grid.

GridUnity is a trusted partner to leading utilities, grid operators, and policymakers. Our technology solutions tackle the interconnection queue backlog by significantly reducing processing times. Whether cutting transmission interconnection timelines from years to months or enabling distribution applications to be processed within a day, we help accelerate energy projects at every scale.

Our Vision
Energizing the planet with a faster, smarter, and more sustainable grid.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

As a Technical Solutions Analyst, you will work directly with utilities and energy innovators to modernize the grid. This customer-facing role connects business needs to the product roadmap and engineering delivery. You will lead discovery, design solutions, support integrations, and guide customers through implementation so they reach value quickly.



This position will report to the VP, Customer Experience.



PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES


DISCOVERY & REQUIREMENTS

 - Understand each customer’s workflows, KPIs, and constraints.

 - Translate business needs into clear, testable requirements.

 - Distinguish between regulatory requirements, customer must-haves, and nice-to-haves.

 - Develop a detailed knowledge of our product to map requirements to existing features, upcoming roadmap items, and needed customizations.


VALUE TRANSLATION

 - Explain the value of solutions in terms of data quality, regulatory compliance, and operational efficiency.




INTEGRATIONS & CONFIGURATION

 - Define how GridUnity and customer systems exchange data.

 - Map fields, identify transformations, and validate data.

 - Guide configuration of GridUnity products and ensure customer IT environments are ready.


IMPLEMENTATION SUPPORT

 - Partner with Project Managers and the CX team to plan and run User Acceptance Testing.

 - Track issues, support defect triage, and manage readiness checklists.

 - Coordinate cutover activities with customers and internal teams.


DOCUMENTATION

 - Work with BAs to document requirements and compare them to market best practices and product capabilities.

 - Create clear source-to-target data mappings, confirm data ownership and quality rules, and review historical data to reduce risk.

 - Identify future integration opportunities.


COMMERCIAL SUPPORT

 - Contribute to Statements of Work and scoping workshops.

 - Act as the technical liaison during sales and early project phases.


CUSTOMER ENABLEMENT

 - Support or deliver product demos and training.
   Ensure clean handoffs to customer administrators and support teams.



WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Foundational understanding of the U.S. transmission interconnection ecosystem.

 - 3–6+ years in a customer-facing solutions role (Solutions/Business Analyst, Solutions Engineer, Implementation Consultant).

 - Strong skills in requirements gathering, process modeling (BPMN/UML or similar), gap analysis, and acceptance criteria.

 - Experience with systems integration concepts: REST APIs, JSON/CSV, authentication, data mapping, and basic ETL.

 - Ability to read payloads, propose data mappings, and identify edge cases.

 - Proficiency in SQL for data validation and test queries.

 - Strong written and verbal communication; able to explain technical issues to non-technical stakeholders and business context to engineers.

 - Experience supporting project execution: issue tracking, risk management, test planning, and tools like Jira/Confluence.

 - Flexible and proactive mindset

source_jobs.title: Project Manager
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

GridUnity is the industry leader in interconnection life cycle management, delivering cutting-edge software solutions that eliminate bottlenecks in the interconnection process and accelerate the transition to a more affordable, sustainable, and resilient energy future.

Our Mission

We unite all grid interconnection stakeholders on a single platform that converts centralized data into actionable intelligence and accelerates the transition to a reliable, sustainable, and cost-efficient electric grid.

GridUnity is a trusted partner to leading utilities, grid operators, and policymakers. Our technology solutions tackle the interconnection queue backlog by significantly reducing processing times. Whether cutting transmission interconnection timelines from years to months or enabling distribution applications to be processed within a day, we help accelerate energy projects at every scale.

Our Vision
Energizing the planet with a faster, smarter, and more sustainable grid.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

As a Project Manager at GridUnity, you will lead end‑to‑end delivery of enterprise SaaS implementations for utility, transmission, and system operator customers. You’ll own scope, schedule, budget, risk, and stakeholder alignment across multi‑phase projects—from discovery and configuration through testing, go‑live, and value realization. You’ll partner closely with customers, Solution Architects, Product, and Engineering to drive adoption of best practices and ensure measurable outcomes.



PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead the successful execution of customer implementation and enhancement projects from initiation through delivery.

 - Develop and manage detailed project plans, timelines, and budgets.

 - Coordinate cross-functional teams—including Product, Engineering, and Customer Success—to ensure alignment on scope and deliverables.

 - Act as a key point of contact for customer stakeholders, providing proactive communication and managing expectations throughout the project lifecycle.

 - Identify and manage risks, issues, and dependencies; escalate and resolve when needed.

 - Track project performance against goals and deliver regular status reports to internal and external stakeholders.

 - Orchestrate UAT, performance testing, and readiness reviews; ensure defects are prioritized and resolved to exit criteria.

 - Drive change management: training plans, stakeholder communications, SOP updates, and post‑go‑live hypercare.

 - Manage contracts/SOWs, baselines, and change requests; track burn, invoicing milestones, and margin.

 - Identify and mitigate delivery risks and cross‑program dependencies; escalate with options and recommendations.

 - Apply project management methodologies and best practices to drive process efficiency and consistency across the CX organization.



WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Bachelor’s degree in Project Management, Business, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience)

 - A few years of project management experience in delivering enterprise SaaS implementations (ideally in the energy or utility sector)

 - Analytical problem-solving skills with a detail-oriented mindset

 - Familiarity with interconnection processes and terminology (e.g., queue management, system impact/facilities studies, DER integration, distribution load connection)

 - Proficiency with project tools: Jira/Confluence (or equivalents), MS Project/Smartsheet, Google Workspace, and collaboration suites

 - Strong stakeholder management and communication skills; comfortable presenting to executive and technical audiences

 - Ability to translate complex policy/engineering requirements into actionable plans; bias for structured problem‑solving and documentation

 - Proven ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously, delivering results on time and within scope



WHAT WILL SET YOU APART

 - Project Management Professional (PMP), Certified Scrum Master (CSM), or equivalent certification

 - Working knowledge of utility/

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager, Market Intelligence & Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

GridUnity is the industry leader in interconnection life cycle management, delivering cutting-edge software solutions that eliminate bottlenecks in the interconnection process and accelerate the transition to a more affordable, sustainable, and resilient energy future.

Our Mission

We unite all grid interconnection stakeholders on a single platform that converts centralized data into actionable intelligence and accelerates the transition to a reliable, sustainable, and cost-efficient electric grid.

GridUnity is a trusted partner to leading utilities, grid operators, and policymakers. Our technology solutions tackle the interconnection queue backlog by significantly reducing processing times. Whether cutting transmission interconnection timelines from years to months or enabling distribution applications to be processed within a day, we help accelerate energy projects at every scale.

Our Vision
Energizing the planet with a faster, smarter, and more sustainable grid.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

As Senior Manager, Market Intelligence & Partnerships, you will serve as a senior individual contributor responsible for shaping GridUnity’s integrated view of business market structure, competitive and partner dynamics, regulatory signals, and customer demand and adoption signals — and translating that understanding into both decision-ready insights for executive leadership and an actionable view of strategic partnerships that advance GridUnity’s position in the market. You will help embed company strategy into GridUnity’s product roadmap, create organizational clarity on strategic direction, and help drive external strategic initiatives, including partnerships.

You will own the Market Map as a strategic decision tool, lead synthesis across competitive and partner intelligence, market signals, and regulatory shifts, and identify and execute product partnerships that reflect GridUnity’s competitive posture and ecosystem strategy. You will partner directly with Product, Sales, Customer Experience, and leadership to ensure GridUnity’s product strategy anticipates market shifts, competitive dynamics, and regulatory change — and that the right external relationships are in place to act on them.

This role carries significant ownership, influence, and cross-functional responsibility.

This position reports to the Director, Product Enablement. This is a senior individual contributor role; leadership is exercised through influence and expertise rather than direct management.




PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES




BUSINESS MARKET & COMPETITIVE INTELLIGENCE

 - Own and maintain GridUnity’s Market Map as a strategic decision tool, providing a clear, structured view of the interconnection and planning ecosystem — including competitive positioning, business structure, ecosystem dynamics, and emerging risks.

 - Develop and maintain competitive intelligence on key players, adjacent solutions, and new entrants, including capabilities, positioning, and potential impact on GridUnity strategy.

 - Identify competitive dynamics, whitespace opportunities, and structural market shifts that inform product direction and differentiation.

 - Define and maintain GridUnity’s “compete, coexist, or integrate” posture across adjacent solution categories, translating ecosystem analysis into clear recommendations for leadership.


INSIGHT INTEGRATION & SYNTHESIS

 - Integrate outputs from competitive intelligence, regulatory monitoring, tariff analysis, Market Radar, and Voice of Customer activities into decision-ready insights for executive leadership.

 - Lead development of market, competitive, customer, and regulatory implication briefs, translating signals into clear product, positioning, and capability implications.

 - Ensure insight quality, consistency, and relevance across the Product Strategy Library, with a focus on decision usability and strategic clarity.


PARTNERSHIP STRATEGY & DEVELOPMENT

 - Develop GridUnity’s product partnership strategy — identi

source_jobs.title: Director of People (Remote)
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

GridUnity is the industry leader in interconnection life cycle management, delivering cutting-edge software solutions that eliminate bottlenecks in the interconnection process and accelerate the transition to a more affordable, sustainable, and resilient energy future.

Our Mission

We unite all grid interconnection stakeholders on a single platform that converts centralized data into actionable intelligence and accelerates the transition to a reliable, sustainable, and cost-efficient electric grid.

GridUnity is a trusted partner to leading utilities, grid operators, and policymakers. Our technology solutions tackle the interconnection queue backlog by significantly reducing processing times. Whether cutting transmission interconnection timelines from years to months or enabling distribution applications to be processed within a day, we help accelerate energy projects at every scale.

Our Vision
Energizing the planet with a faster, smarter, and more sustainable grid.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re seeking a Director of People who thrives in the dynamic realities of a scaling SaaS company. You will partner closely with the CFO and the Operations Team, comprising five additional people, to strengthen, standardize, and scale our people systems.

This role requires a true player coach mentality, blending strategy with hands-on execution. It’s ideal for someone who enjoys building, improving, and operationalizing HR processes.

As GridUnity scales, this role can evolve to include greater ownership and leadership responsibilities within People Operations.




TEAM DYNAMICS

Today this is a 1 person People team, but there is 1 additional budgeted People headcount who this person will manage. This role is responsible for strengthening the function’s structure, accountability, and scalability. As we grow, you will assess team design and resource needs to ensure the People & Talent department is built for long-term success.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

Our business is scaling. Now we need our people systems to scale with the same clarity and discipline.

We’re looking for someone who will:

 - Bring cohesion and consistency across the full employee lifecycle

 - Design scalable systems that support growth without adding unnecessary complexity

 - Elevate manager capability through thoughtful performance and development frameworks

 - Reinforce and evolve our culture as the organization grows

 - Create clarity, accountability, and fairness in how people decisions are made
   
   

Above all, the successful candidate brings grit, resilience, and a curious, solutions-oriented mindset — someone who sees ambiguity as an opportunity to build, not a signal that something is broken.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


PEOPLE OPERATIONS

 - Partner with the CFO and the Operations Team to set HR priorities, translate strategy into action, and measure progress

 - Audit and refine HR processes, documentation, and workflows to create a cohesive operating model

 - Provide guidance, structure, and support to the HR Manager while strengthening team capacity

 - Champion our values throughout all people programs and communication

 - Support assessment and selection of future HRIS or relevant HR software
   


TALENT ACQUISITION

 - Lead full-cycle recruiting alongside the HR Manager in the US (intake, sourcing, assessments, scheduling, offers, close). Ashby ATS experience is a plus

 - Improve and edit our recruiting playbook for scalability and determine long-term resourcing once it’s established

 - Partner with our department heads to define role requirements, competencies, and success profiles, and lead active recruiting

 - Ensure hiring is aligned with GridUnity’s values and stage and can support SaaS scaling needs
   


PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT & COMPENSATION LEVELING

 - Refine or redesign the performance management cycle

 - Evaluate the effectiveness of our current compensation leveling system and propose improvements

 - Leverage Pave and sim

notes: Software company in energy sector with few job postings
